symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe unrelenting rest pain in the forearm/hand

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden severe rest pain is the first of the 6 Ps of acute arterial occlusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pallor / waxy-white hand progressing to mottling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pallor progressing to mottling is a defining sign of arterial inflow occlusion; patient-observable over video.
DVT causes a warm, red, swollen limb — not a pale/mottled one.

symptoms.symptom_name: Perishing cold limb with temperature difference from the other side

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Coldness reflects absent arterial perfusion — one of the 6 Ps.
Venous thrombosis does not cool the limb; arterial occlusion does.

symptoms.symptom_name: Paraesthesia progressing to numbness with grip weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sensorimotor deficit upgrades the limb to Rutherford IIb (immediately threatened) — a marker of emergent salvage need.
Paraesthesia alone could be radicular, but the accompanying pallor/coldness/mottling makes it vascular.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent radial, ulnar and brachial pulses on the right

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pulselessness confirms arterial occlusion — clinician-palpated, NOT obtainable via telehealth; sealed answer-key sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transverse temperature-demarcation line at the mid-forearm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A demarcation line localises the level of occlusion — a clinician bedside finding, not remotely obtainable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Collapsed superficial veins in the affected limb

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Empty/collapsed veins (venous guttering) reflect low arterial inflow — a clinician inspection finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Irregularly irregular pulse on auscultation (atrial fibrillation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the embolic source at the bedside; the AI Doctor infers AF from reported history, not from auscultation.
AF is the commonest source of a peripheral arterial embolus; it powerfully raises pre-test probability of an embolic occlusion.
Interrupted anticoagulation in AF sharply raises thromboembolic risk and explains the acute embolic event.

symptoms.symptom_name: No preceding trauma or fracture to the limb

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compartment syndrome usually follows trauma, fracture or reperfusion; its absence makes primary compartment syndrome unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Arterial duplex / CT angiography showing abrupt cessation of brachial artery flow

investigation_details.investigation_name: Arterial duplex ultrasound or CT angiography of the upper limb
investigation_details.expected_result: Abrupt cut-off of contrast/flow in the brachial artery consistent with embolic occlusion
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Level and cause of occlusion unconfirmed — but imaging must not delay emergency revascularisation for an immediately-threatened limb.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging confirms the occlusion level for operative planning; a hospital investigation never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: The 6 Ps of acute limb ischaemia
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Pain, Pallor, Pulselessness, Paraesthesia, Perishing cold and Paralysis together are the classic and near-specific picture of acute arterial occlusion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: An immediately-threatened limb needs emergency revascularisation; the disposition, not remote testing, is the priority.
Confirms level and cause of occlusion for operative planning — performed in hospital, must not delay revascularisation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: ECG and cardiac work-up for embolic source
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Documents AF and guides anticoagulation once the limb is addressed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Remote analgesia titration or watchful waiting before escalation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Any remote temporising that delays 000 escalation wastes the narrow salvage window — an error of commission.
